title: [PrepareForEmission] Remove unnecessary tempoary introduced by bitcast op
body:
This avoids creating unnecessary temporary wires because of term size limit. 

```mlir
module attributes {circt.loweringOptions = "maximumNumberOfTermsPerExpression=2"} {
  hw.module @Foo(%in_0: i4, %in_1: i4, %in_2: i4, %in_3: i4) -> (out: !hw.array<4xi4>) {
    %0 = comb.concat %in_0, %in_1, %in_2, %in_3 : i4, i4, i4, i4
    %1 = hw.bitcast %0 : (i16) -> !hw.array<4xi4>
    hw.output %1 : !hw.array<4xi4>
  }
}
```

For the example above, `%0` is spilled because the term size is 4 but however `%0` is only used by an assignment through bitcast op. 
Before:
```verilog
  wire [15:0] _GEN = {in_0, in_1, in_2, in_3};	// foo.mlir:2:10
  assign out = /*cast(bit[3:0][3:0])*/_GEN;	// foo.mlir:2:10, :3:10, :4:5
```
After:
```verilog
  assign out = /*cast(bit[3:0][3:0])*/{in_0, in_1, in_2, in_3};	// foo.mlir:5:10, :6:10, :7:5
```
